channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: American utility firm Itron discloses breach of internal IT network
item.description: Itron, Inc. has disclosed, via an 8-K filing with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), a cybersecurity incident in which an unauthorized third party accessed certain internal systems. [....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft rolls out revamped Windows Insider Program
item.description: Microsoft says it's rolling out a revamped Windows Insider Program experience as part of the broader plans to address performance and reliability concerns affecting Windows 11. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Threat actor uses Microsoft Teams to deploy new “Snow” malware
item.description: A threat group tracked as UNC6692 uses social engineering to deploy a new, custom malware suite named 'Snow' which includes a browser extension, a tunneler, and a backdoor. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Researchers Uncover Pre-Stuxnet ‘fast16’ Malware Targeting Engineering Software
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new Lua-based malware created years before the notorious Stuxnet worm that aimed to sabotage Iran's nuclear program by destroying uranium enrichment centrif...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA Adds 4 Exploited Flaws to KEV, Sets May 2026 Federal Deadline
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Friday added four vulnerabilities impacting SimpleHelp, Samsung MagicINFO 9 Server, and D-Link DIR-823X series routers to its Known ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: ADT confirms data breach after ShinyHunters leak threat
item.description: Home security giant ADT has confirmed a data breach after the ShinyHunters extortion group threatened to leak stolen data unless a ransom is paid. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Helping Romance Scam Victims Require a Proactive, Empathic Approach
item.description: People targeted by confidence schemes find getting help is a lonely road. Experts want law enforcement, financial and government institutions to work together and protect them....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Firestarter malware survives Cisco firewall updates, security patches
item.description: Cybersecurity agencies in the U.S. and U.K. are warning about a custom malware called Firestarter persisting on Cisco Firepower and Secure Firewall devices running Adaptive Security Appliance (ASA) or...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Windows Update gets new controls to reduce forced restarts
item.description: Microsoft is rolling out Windows Update improvements that give users more control over how updates are installed while reducing disruption from frequent or poorly timed restarts. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New BlackFile extortion group linked to surge of vishing attacks
item.description: A new financially motivated hacking group tracked as BlackFile has been linked to a wave of data theft and extortion attacks against retail and hospitality organizations since February 2026. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft to roll out Entra passkeys on Windows in late April
item.description: Microsoft will roll out passkey support for phishing-resistant passwordless authentication to Microsoft Entra‑protected resources from Windows devices starting late April. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New ‘Pack2TheRoot’ flaw gives hackers root Linux access
item.description: A new vulnerability dubbed Pack2TheRoot could be exploited in the PackageKit daemon to allow local Linux users to install or remove system packages and gain root permissions. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: FIRESTARTER Backdoor Hit Federal Cisco Firepower Device, Survives Security Patches
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has revealed that an unnamed federal civilian agency's Cisco Firepower device running Adaptive Security Appliance (ASA) software was co...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: US Busts Myanmar Ring Targeting US Citizens in Financial Fraud
item.description: Some 29 people were charged, including a Cambodian senator, and authorities seized more than 500 Web domains tied to fake investment sites....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Glasswing Secured the Code. The Rest of Your Stack Is Still on You
item.description: Forgotten integrations, shadow IT, SaaS, and now shadow AI and agents are everywhere, and attackers don't need sophisticated AI models to take advantage....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: NASA Employees Duped in Chinese Phishing Scheme Targeting U.S. Defense Software
item.description: The Office of Inspector General (OIG) of the U.S. National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) has revealed how a Chinese national posed as a U.S. researcher as part of a spear-phishing campai...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: DORA and operational resilience: Credential management as a financial risk control
item.description: Article 9 of DORA makes authentication and access control a legal obligation for EU financial entities. Here is what the regulation requires, and what a breach looks like when those controls are missi...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Over 10,000 Zimbra servers vulnerable to ongoing XSS attacks
item.description: Over 10,000 Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS) instances exposed online are vulnerable to ongoing attacks exploiting a cross-site scripting (XSS) security flaw. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI Phishing Is No. 1 With a Bullet for Cyberattackers
item.description: In the past six months, companies have seen a significant influx of AI-powered phishing, as cyberattackers progress from small campaigns to 1-to-1 personalized attacks....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: North Korea's Lazarus Targets macOS Users via ClickFix
item.description: Lazarus continues leveraging ClickFix for initial access and data theft, in this case, against Mac-centric organizations and their high-value leaders....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Bridging the AI Agent Authority Gap: Continuous Observability as the Decision Engine
item.description: The AI Agent Authority Gap - From Ungoverned to Delegation As discussed in our previous article, AI agents are exposing a structural gap in enterprise security, but the problem is often framed too nar...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 26 FakeWallet Apps Found on Apple App Store Targeting Crypto Seed Phrases
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a set of malicious apps on the Apple App Store that impersonate popular cryptocurrency wallets in an attempt to steal recovery phrases and private keys since ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft now lets admins uninstall Copilot on enterprise devices
item.description: Microsoft says IT administrators can now uninstall the AI-powered Copilot digital assistant from enterprise devices using a new policy setting, which has become broadly available after the April 2026 ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Tropic Trooper Uses Trojanized SumatraPDF and GitHub to Deploy AdaptixC2
item.description: Chinese-speaking individuals are the target of a new campaign that uses a trojanized version of SumatraPDF reader to deploy the AdaptixC2 Beacon post-exploitation agent and ultimately facilitate the a...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: LMDeploy CVE-2026-33626 Flaw Exploited Within 13 Hours of Disclosure
item.description: A high-severity security flaw in LMDeploy, an open-source toolkit for compressing, deploying, and serving LLMs, has come under active exploitation in the wild less than 13 hours after its public discl...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Tropic Trooper APT Takes Aim at Home Routers, Japanese Targets
item.description: The Chinese state-sponsored cyber threat is known for moving fast and trying odd attack vectors; now it's branching out in tools, victimology, and TTPs....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Chinese APT Abuses Multiple Cloud Tools to Spy on Mongolia
item.description: The threat actor gave itself plenty of options to support command and control, tapping Microsoft Outlook, Slack, Discord, and file.io for online espionage....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Hackers exploit file upload bug in Breeze Cache WordPress plugin
item.description: Hackers are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability in the Breeze Cache plugin for WordPress that allows uploading arbitrary files on the server without authentication. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: China-Backed Hackers Are Industrializing Botnets
item.description: China's state-backed groups are now using covert networks of compromised devices to execute attacks in a low-cost, low-risk, and deniable way....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Bitwarden CLI npm package compromised to steal developer credentials
item.description: The Bitwarden CLI was briefly compromised after attackers uploaded a malicious @bitwarden/cli package to npm containing a credential-stealing payload capable of spreading to other projects. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
